title: Communication loss and partial digital blindness
summary: The greenhouse has lost reliable communication visibility for part of the control layer, making actuator state and telemetry trust uncertain during normal production conditions.
suspected_issue: Partial communication loss is reducing trust in telemetry and actuator state visibility.
symptoms: The gateway heartbeat appears delayed or unstable.
Recent telemetry cannot be fully trusted for real-time operational decisions.
The current physical state of some climate actuators cannot be confirmed with confidence.
The crop remains in active production conditions while operational visibility is degraded.

observed_signals.interpretation: The gateway heartbeat exceeds the expected range and suggests degraded communication reliability.
Humidity seems elevated, but confidence in full system visibility is reduced.
Temperature appears normal, but the signal is only partially trusted due to communication instability.

operational_context.note: The greenhouse remains in normal production mode, but communication reliability is degraded.
operational_context.impact: The service must avoid overconfidence because some digital visibility is uncertain.

operational_context.note: No explicit backup control path is confirmed for the affected communication layer.
operational_context.impact: The service should favor human verification and cautious action over confident automation.

agronomic_context.note: Production-stage tomato crop may be affected if climate deviation persists without reliable visibility.
agronomic_context.impact: Delayed confirmation of actuator state could increase crop stress and disease exposure.

business_context.note: Loss of trusted visibility creates operational risk even before full failure is confirmed.
business_context.impact: The case should be prioritized because uncertainty itself has business impact.

notes: This case is designed to test how Agro-DO behaves when severity is high but operational confidence is weakened by limited visibility and partially untrusted signals.
